title: Monotone-Constrained Dry Expert With Purged Chronological Stacking
summary: Official model docs note monotonic constraints can improve predictive performance when prior structure is strong.
rationale: Official model docs note monotonic constraints can improve predictive performance when prior structure is strong. For dry green-flag racing, features such as stop-pressure, inability to finish, and legality pressure should usually move pit probability in one direction, while recent-pit freshness should move it the other. Pairing that with purged chronological validation and sigmoid stacking gives a lower-variance specialist.
implementation_hint: Train a dry-race LightGBM/XGBoost expert with increasing constraints on `Service_Window_Pressure`, `Finish_Stop_Pressure`, `Cannot_Finish_On_Current_Tyre`, and legality-pressure features, and decreasing constraints on cooldown/freshness features. Evaluate with race-ordered splits plus a gap/purge around fold boundaries, then blend OOF predictions using sigmoid calibration or logistic stacking rather than isotonic.
expected_effect: Small but robust generalization gain by removing nonsensical inversions and reducing fold-specific overfit, especially if the leaderboard test period shifts slightly from train.
risk: Bad constraint signs can suppress real overcut regimes, and constrained trees can become too shallow if the feature set is noisy.
